It's worth taking a moment to talk about how to use chat GBT responsibly. While it's a powerful tool, it's still just that a tool and it works best when we pair it with thoughtful human oversight. Start by being mindful of what you put into chat GBT. Avoid sharing personal, confidential or proprietary information unless you're using a workspace. With enterprise privacy protections and your organization's policies, allow it. It's also important to keep a human in the loop, especially for important or high stakes work chat GT can sometimes be inaccurate or outdated because it's generating responses based on patterns of its training data, not live knowledge. Always double check critical facts with trusted sources. And another thing to watch out for is bias or perspective. Since the model learns from data created by humans, some of these human biases can show up in its responses, treat outputs as drafts and read them critically adjusting language when needed For anything that involves legal, medical, or financial decisions. Chat GBT should never be your final stop. It's not a licensed expert. And its advice won't always be specific to your situation. If your school or workspace expects you to disclose when AI tools are being used, keep logs or links to your conversation so others can see how chat GBT contributed. And then finally, you might have heard about or even experienced something called a hallucination. This is a known limitation of all LLMs and AI systems. A hallucination is when the AI produces something that sounds believable, but it's actually incorrect. Like when a friend is really confidently telling you a story that they believe is true, but some of the details aren't quite right, you can't avoid hallucinations altogether.
